objective: Fix startup failure in non-GPU platform checks by adding automatic CPU device fallback only when DEVICE is not explicitly set and CUDA runtime is unavailable.
scope: Apply a minimal runtime-safe change in src/engine_args.py to probe CUDA availability safely, force device=cpu only for implicit-device flows when CUDA is unavailable, and keep multi-GPU tensor-parallel auto behavior gated on actual GPU availability.
acceptance_criteria: When DEVICE is explicitly set, engine args keep that value with no automatic override.
When DEVICE is not explicitly set and CUDA runtime is unavailable (is_available false, device_count 0, or CUDA probe raises), engine args set device=cpu and log why.
When CUDA runtime is available, device auto behavior remains unchanged and no CPU forcing occurs.
Automatic multi-GPU tensor_parallel_size adjustment happens only when CUDA runtime is available and GPU count > 1.
rollback_plan: Fork-local rollback is a single commit revert of src/engine_args.py and this request file, restoring previous DEVICE auto behavior if unexpected CPU fallback side effects are observed.
deploy_plan: Deploy with conservative rollout 1->2->4; verify startup logs show CPU fallback only in non-GPU checks and unchanged CUDA path on real GPU nodes.
threat_model: Primary risk is unintended CPU execution in GPU-capable environments or hidden CUDA probe regressions; mitigated by honoring explicit DEVICE, requiring successful CUDA probe before GPU-only behaviors, and log visibility of fallback reason.
